text: told the interviewer my greatest weakness was 'caring too much' and he literally laughed at me
my resume says i know python. i googled how to print hello world yesterday
linkedin guy congratulated me on my 'work anniversary' at a job i got fired from 2 months ago
went to a networking event and just stood by the cheese table for 3 hours
cover letter said i was passionate about synergy. i dont even know what synergy means
recruiter ghosted me after 6 rounds of interviews. six. SIX.
got passed over for promotion again. the new guy starts monday and i have to train him lmao
my career pivot from accounting to pottery lasted exactly 11 days
interviewer asked where i see myself in 5 years and i said 'not here' by accident
put 'proficient in excel' on my resume. i can make the cells different colors thats about it
some dude on linkedin wrote a 14 paragraph post about finding a quarter on the ground. called it leadership
networking is just standing in a room lying to strangers while holding a warm beer
wrote in my cover letter that i 'thrive in fast-paced environments' im literally napping rn
recruiter said theyd get back to me in a week. that was in 2024
accidentally sent my cover letter addressed to the wrong company. got the interview anyway??
career coach told me to 'follow my passion' bro my passion is sleeping
the promotion went to someone who's been here 3 months. i've been here 7 years. cool cool cool
my resume has a 2 year gap that i explain as 'freelance consulting' i was playing elden ring
interviewer asked me to sell him this pen and i just stared at it for 45 seconds
linkedin influencer posted 'i fired my top performer to teach the team humility' what
went to career fair in a suit that was 2 sizes too big. looked like a kid wearing dads clothes
cover letter: 'i would be a great asset to your team' me irl: cries in the bathroom at work
pivoted from law to tech to art to law again. my linkedin looks like a conspiracy board
recruiter called me about a job, asked my salary expectations, then hung up laughing
my boss said i wasnt 'leadership material' then asked me to plan the entire office party
put 'team player' on every resume ive ever written. i hate teams. i hate playing.
interview went great until i called the ceo by the wrong name. twice.
spent 4 hours on a cover letter for a job that pays 35k. this is fine
networking event pro tip: the bathroom is the only honest place in the building
linkedin post: 'i wake up at 4am to meditate and journal' bro you sell insurance
got rejected from a job i didnt even apply to. they found my resume online and said no thanks
career pivot into coding. week 3 and i just mass deleted a production database
resume says 'managed a team of 12' it was a minecraft server
promotion meeting lasted 4 minutes. 3 of those minutes were small talk about weather
recruiter emailed me about a 'rockstar developer' position. pay was 40k. in san francisco.
wrote 'detail-oriented' on my resume with a typo in the word oriented
interviewer said 'we're like a family here' and i immediately wanted to leave
my cover letter starts with 'to whom it may concern' because i genuinely do not know who it concerns
pivoted from teacher to content creator. turns out nobody wants content about 7th grade math
at a networking dinner and the guy next to me is pitching me an mlm. again.
linkedin said 87 people viewed my profile this week. 87 people saw me and said nah
got promoted to 'senior' title. same pay same desk same existential dread
recruiter asked if i was 'open to relocation' to a city that doesnt have a grocery store
resume gap explanation: 'personal development' aka i watched every show on netflix
interview asked for my biggest accomplishment and my mind went completely blank. just static.
linkedin guy posted his rejection letter and called it 'a gift from the universe' bro its a no
cover letter said i have 'excellent communication skills' then i stuttered thru the whole interview
tried to pivot into UX design. turns out having opinions about fonts isnt a qualification
networking events are just halloween for adults. everyone pretending to be something theyre not
got passed over for a promotion by someone who started after me. they said she had 'fresh energy'
my resume is 4 pages long because i keep adding fake hobbies to seem interesting
recruiter said the role was 'entry level' but required 8 years experience. math isnt mathing
interview question: 'if you were an animal what would you be' i panicked and said slug
linkedin influencer: 'i let an intern fire me for a day. heres what happened.' no you didnt
wrote a cover letter so good i almost believed it myself. almost.
career counselor said i should 'lean in' and i literally fell out of my chair
my resume says bilingual. i know english and enough spanish to order tacos
promotion review: 'you need to be more visible.' i sit directly in front of your office karen
went to a networking brunch and someone handed me a business card made of wood. WOOD.
recruiter said 'competitive salary' and it was competing with minimum wage apparently
pivoted from finance to wellness coaching. now im broke AND stressed but in yoga pants
cover letter: 'i am confident i can bring value.' narrator: he could not.
got asked to do a presentation as part of the interview. 20 slides. for an internship. unpaid.
linkedin dude humble bragging about turning down 6 job offers. sir this is a wendys
my biggest career achievement is learning how to cry silently in open plan offices
resume says 'self-starter' but my alarm goes off 9 times every morning
interview went so bad the hiring manager walked me out early 'to save us both time'
somebody on linkedin called getting laid off 'a chance to rebrand' im begging you to stop
networking tip: always have a drink in your hand so nobody tries to make you shake theirs
cover letter said i left my last job for 'new challenges.' the challenge was not screaming daily
career pivot attempt #4. at this point my career is just a series of u-turns
recruiter called me 'a great culture fit' for a company whose culture is apparently unpaid overtime
put leadership experience on my resume from being the oldest sibling. desperate times
promotion went to the guy who brings donuts every friday. maybe i shouldve bought donuts
interview tip: dont tell them your hobby is true crime podcasts. they get weird about it
linkedin post: 'i dont have a degree and i make 500k.' comments: 'my dad owns the company'
resume says project manager. i managed to project confidence for about 3 minutes
tried to network at a conference and accidentally joined a line for the bathroom instead
cover letter: 'i thrive under pressure.' reality: i cry when the printer jams
pivoted from journalism to marketing. same lying different paycheck
recruiter hit me up on linkedin for a job at my own company. they didnt even notice
got told i was 'overqualified' which is corporate for 'we cant afford you but wont pay more'
linkedin guy posted a selfie crying at his desk and called it 'vulnerability in leadership'
my resume font is the most thought out part of my entire career honestly
networking event had a trust fall exercise. i just went home. immediately.
interview asked about salary expectations and i accidentally said my rent amount instead
cover letter: 'im excited about this opportunity' translation: im excited about rent money
career pivot from nursing to tech. miss the patients. dont miss the 12 hour shifts tho
been employee of the month 0 times in 6 years. consistent king
panel interview with 7 people. i blacked out. apparently i got the job??
resume gap: 2020-2021. interviewer: say no more. me: thank god
linkedin says congrats on 10 years in your field. 10 years of what exactly
networking is just collecting anxiety in a room full of strangers with lanyards
cover letter generator wrote something better than me in 4 seconds. im the robot now
quiet promotion: more work same title same pay new responsibilities. blessed
interviewer asked about my 5 year plan. bold of them to assume i plan
linkedin guru said quit your job and follow your dreams. my dream is health insurance
career fair table had candy. i took candy and left. efficient networking
was told im overqualified. sir i cant even parallel park
asked for a raise. got a pizza party. the cheese was cold
